creator: swhetzel@gmail.com
text:
Manufacturer: HP
Model: Pavilion dv8135nr  (http://h10025.www1.hp.com/ewfrf/wc/document?docname=c00585596&lc=en&cc=us&lang=en&y=6&x=5&dlc=en&product=1818492)
Chipset/Socket: ATI Technologies Inc
Submitter: Scot Hetzel (swhetzel@gmail.com)
Tested FreeBSD Versions: 6.1-RELEASE, 6.1-STABLE thur 6.2-Beta2, 7.0-CURRENT
 
 Notes:
 This Laptop will not boot correctly from a FreeBSD/amd64 install CD (tried 6.1-RC, and 6.2-Beta2 boot CDs), as the boot loader crashes, and continuosly spams the display with unreadable hex dump.  To install FreeBSD/amd64 on this laptop you will need to press the spacebar until the system beeps or when you see "/boot/kernel/kernel".  The system has no problems booting off the Hard Drive.

Currently there are only a couple of devices that are not supported by native FreeBSD drivers:

ndis0: <Broadcom 802.11g Network Adapter> mem 0xc0204000-0xc0205fff irq 21 at device 2.0 on pci6
ndis0: NDIS API version: 5.1

none0@pci0:20:6:        class=0x070300 card=0x309b103c chip=0x43781002 rev=0x02 hdr=0x00
    vendor   = 'ATI Technologies Inc'
    class    = simple comms
    subclass = generic modem
none1@pci6:4:3: class=0x018000 card=0x309b103c chip=0x8033104c rev=0x00 hdr=0x00
    vendor   = 'Texas Instruments (TI)'
    device   = 'PCIxx21 Integrated FlashMedia Controller'
    class    = mass storage
none2@pci6:4:4: class=0x080500 card=0x309b103c chip=0x8034104c rev=0x00 hdr=0x00
    vendor   = 'Texas Instruments (TI)'
    class    = base peripheral

Fix: 

This probably belongs on it's own page:

   FreeBSD/amd64 Project -- Laptops

Where we could list the supported Laptops, and known work arounds to get FreeBSD/amd64 installed on them.
